steps.text: Define variables: $x$ = number of 1-point questions, $y$ = number of 2-point questions. Each 1-point question contributes $1 \cdot x$ points; each 2-point question contributes $2 \cdot y$ points.
The total points from both question types must equal $78$: $x + 2y = 78$.

Check the other choices: $xy = 78$ is a product, not a sum of contributions. $3(x+y) = 78$ implies each question is worth 3 points on average. $x + y = 78$ counts the number of questions, not the point values.
Only $x + 2y = 78$ correctly weights each question type by its point value and sums to the total. That matches choice D — D is correct.

choices.text: • $xy = 78$ multiplies the counts together rather than summing their point contributions. This has no relevant meaning for the total points. Eliminated.
• $3(x+y) = 78$ means $x + y = 26$ total questions each worth 3 points on average — not how the scoring is defined. Eliminated.
• $x + y = 78$ would mean the total number of questions is 78, ignoring the different point values. Eliminated.
• $x + 2y = 78$: 1-point questions contribute $x$ points and 2-point questions contribute $2y$ points, summing to 78. That matches choice D — D is correct.
